title: How tool calling works
description: The model proposes a tool name and JSON arguments; the application executes and returns a result, looping until the model stops calling tools.
body: Use when implementing or debugging function/tool calling. The model does not run the tool: it emits a structured request (name + JSON arguments) which the application validates and executes, then returns the result as a new message; the model may chain further calls. Do not treat the model's proposed arguments as trusted or assume the tool actually ran, and avoid infinite call loops. Actions: validate arguments against the tool schema, execute under permission checks, return a typed result or error, and cap the number of tool turns. Validation signals include tests for malformed arguments, a denied side-effecting call, and a loop cap. Risk boundary: executing a side-effecting tool on unvalidated model arguments is a critical finding.
